prompt:
Anatomical muscle-activation diagram of the "单腿深蹲" (pistol squat / single-leg squat) exercise, side view, facing right. Person posture: Athletic person at the bottom of a single-leg squat (pistol squat): right leg supporting, right knee deeply bent so right thigh is parallel to the floor or slightly below, right foot flat on the ground; left leg fully extended straight forward off the ground, parallel to the floor; both arms extended forward at shoulder height for counterbalance; torso leaning forward with neutral spine; plain black athletic shorts and gray tank top. Body rendered with semi-transparent skin so underlying muscles are visible. Highlight in saturated red the primary working muscles: quadriceps and gluteus maximus of the supporting leg. Highlight in orange the secondary stabilizers: hamstring group and gluteus medius of the supporting hip and core stabilizers. Other muscles in pale neutral gray. Add small clean labels with thin black arrow leader lines pointing from each label to its target muscle group. The labels MUST be in simplified Chinese characters using a clean sans-serif font. Use exactly the following labels: "股四头肌" pointing to the quadriceps of the supporting leg; "臀大肌" pointing to the gluteus maximus of the supporting leg; "臀中肌 / 核心" pointing to the gluteus medius and core area. 

CRITICAL CONSTRAINTS — these are non-negotiable:
1. ALL TEXT IN THE IMAGE MUST BE SIMPLIFIED CHINESE CHARACTERS ONLY.
2. ABSOLUTELY NO ENGLISH WORDS, NO LATIN LETTERS, NO PINYIN ANYWHERE IN THE IMAGE.
3. NO romanization or transliteration of muscle names.
4. NO captions, NO subtitles, NO step-by-step text overlays.
5. ONLY the muscle labels listed above should appear as text.

Style: clean medical textbook illustration, off-white background, no facial features (head shape may be shown but no eyes/nose/mouth), anatomically accurate proportions, no shadows on background, no watermarks, no signature.
